summary: This notebook implements an inference pipeline that ensembles three distinct sensor-fusion models for gesture recognition. It combines a TensorFlow CNN-RNN architecture, a PyTorch CNN-BERT model, and a PyTorch CNN-BiLSTM model with explicit signal processing. Each model family is loaded across multiple cross-validation folds, and their output probabilities are averaged to produce the final prediction. The pipeline emphasizes custom quaternion-based motion dynamics, region-wise TOF statistics, and sequence padding to capture temporal patterns in multi-modal sensor data.
competition_flow: Loads pre-trained models from Kaggle datasets, applies custom sensor-derived features and sequence padding to input data, generates predictions from each model, averages them, and outputs gesture probabilities.
data_reading: Reads raw sensor data via polars and pandas from /kaggle/input/cmi-detect-behavior-with-sensor-data and precomputed CSV datasets.
Converts polars DataFrames to pandas for feature computation and model input.
data_processing: Pads sequences to the 95th percentile length.
Applies StandardScaler normalization across IMU, thermal, and TOF channels.
Handles missing values with forward/backward fill and scaled NaN placeholders.
Applies MixUp augmentation (alpha=0.4) and Gaussian noise injection during training.
Removes gravity from accelerometer data using quaternion rotation matrices.
features_engineering: Linear acceleration (gravity removed), linear acceleration magnitude, and linear acceleration jerk.
Angular velocity (x, y, z) and angular distance computed from rotation quaternions.
TOF region statistics (mean, std, min, max) across 5 sensors and multiple region splits.
IMU magnitude, jerk, and energy/power features.
LPF/HPF filtered IMU signals and FIR filtered IMU signals.
cv_strategy: StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5) and StratifiedGroupKFold(n_splits=10) across different model folds
ensembling: Averages prediction probabilities from three distinct model families (TensorFlow CNN-RNN, PyTorch CNN-BERT, PyTorch CNN-BiLSTM) loaded across multiple folds, with no additional post-processing.
insights: Custom quaternion-based gravity removal and angular distance calculation effectively capture motion dynamics for gesture recognition.
TOF sensor data benefits from region-wise statistical aggregation rather than raw pixel input.
BERT can be effectively adapted for multivariate time-series sensor fusion via CLS token concatenation.
Ensemble averaging across diverse architectures (CNN-RNN vs CNN-Transformer) improves robustness.
Signal processing techniques like FIR filtering and LPF/HPF on IMU data enhance feature representation for downstream neural networks.
